channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Charlotte Hornets
channel.description: Charlotte Hornets trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana announced that …
item.description: There’s another Ball in the bunch. LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana announced that they had welcomed a baby boy into the world earlier this year and that they are launching a platform and nonprofit in order to support women through the fertility and IVF process. The new addition to the family was born in January and the couple told People in a statement that their son, LaOne, “has changed our hearts in the best way.” “Family has always meant everything to us, but becoming parents has give

item.title: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana are officially …
item.description: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana are officially parents. The NBA star, 24, and the model, whose real name is Analicia Chaves, have welcomed their first baby together, son LaOne, a rep for the couple confirms to PEOPLE exclusively. Ball and Montana, who welcomed their son this past January, are also launching I Am Fertility, a platform and nonprofit dedicated to supporting women navigating fertility and IVF journeys.

item.title: Obviously, I’m not saying that we’re perfect or that …
item.description: Nick Wright got a lot of exposure for calling LaMelo Ball a clown, specifically. He said he’s unserious, and LaMelo has had that “unserious” label for quite a few years. I’d say this year is the first year where people started to go, “Oh, wait a minute.” What’s it like to hear your guard talked about so negatively in the press? Do you hear it, and do you think anything of it? Moussa Diabaté: I mean, it’s one of those things where most people are not in the locker room. Everybody is going to hav

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana announced that …
item.description: There’s another Ball in the bunch. LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana announced that they had welcomed a baby boy into the world earlier this year and that they are launching a platform and nonprofit in order to support women through the fertility and IVF process. The new addition to the family was born in January and the couple told People in a statement that their son, LaOne, “has changed our hearts in the best way.” “Family has always meant everything to us, but becoming parents has give

item.title: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana are officially …
item.description: LaMelo Ball and his partner Ana Montana are officially parents. The NBA star, 24, and the model, whose real name is Analicia Chaves, have welcomed their first baby together, son LaOne, a rep for the couple confirms to PEOPLE exclusively. Ball and Montana, who welcomed their son this past January, are also launching I Am Fertility, a platform and nonprofit dedicated to supporting women navigating fertility and IVF journeys.

item.title: Obviously, I’m not saying that we’re perfect or that …
item.description: Nick Wright got a lot of exposure for calling LaMelo Ball a clown, specifically. He said he’s unserious, and LaMelo has had that “unserious” label for quite a few years. I’d say this year is the first year where people started to go, “Oh, wait a minute.” What’s it like to hear your guard talked about so negatively in the press? Do you hear it, and do you think anything of it? Moussa Diabaté: I mean, it’s one of those things where most people are not in the locker room. Everybody is going to hav

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: In other words, 94 percent of the picks in June’s …
item.description: But here at TrueHoop, we just crunched decades of numbers, and discovered that the likelihood any team will draft a player, and have that player become an excellent NBA performer while still in the same uniform, is six percent. In other words, 94 percent of the picks in June’s draft are likely not to make your dreams come true, either because: * They’re not good enough, injured, under-nurtured, or similar; * Or by the time they’re highly productive, they’ve moved on to another team. We used Dunk

item.title: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams …
item.description: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams considering a deal with streaming platform Victory+. Sources also told SBJ last month that Fubo makes surprise pitch to all 13 NBA teams that fled Main Street. DAZN, Gray TV and Nexstar have also been reported as local options for teams. The Pistons’ former RSN also was the home to the Tigers and Red Wings, but those clubs – both owned by the Ilitch family -- launched Detroit SportsNet in collaboration with MLB Media. That first-of-its-kind,

item.title: “Instead of fining teams for tanking, we need to strip …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Grant Williams on how to fix tanking: “Instead of fining teams for tanking, we need to strip them of the lottery rights.”

item.title: So, yes, you could hear about the framework of a …
item.description: Technically, the Heat isn’t eligible to trade its 2026 first-round pick (No. 13 overall) because NBA rules prevent teams from being without a first-round selection in two consecutive years (the Heat sent a top-14 protected first-round 2027 pick to the Charlotte Hornets in the Rozier trade). But the Heat could pick a player on behalf of another team at No. 13 in the first round of this year’s draft as part of a prearranged trade. This means the Heat and Bucks could agree to a trade prior to next

item.title: Michigan’s Nimari Burnett has garnered high NBA …
item.description: Jake Weingarten: Michigan’s Nimari Burnett has garnered high NBA interest and has several NBA workouts, including the Washington Wizards and Charlotte Hornets next week, a source told @stockrisers. He will have several NBA workouts following that at the end of May and into June.

item.title: “I went into the morning when they announced it …
item.description: Kon Knueppel on Rookie of the Year award: “Obviously, I wanted to win it. If you would have told me at the beginning of the season that this is how my year would go, I would have taken it one hundred percent. “I went into the morning when they announced it thinking, ‘I can’t do anything about it now. I did all I could.’ I would have felt worse if I felt like I had left something out there, but I felt like I did all I could. “Maybe I wish I could have shot it better at the end of the season, but

item.title: Kon Knueppel continued his podcast tour this summer, …
item.description: Former Duke star Kon Knueppel continued his podcast tour this summer, making an appearance on the "Dale Jr. Download," hosted by none other than former two-time Daytona 500 winner Dale Earnhardt Jr. This comes not long after appearing on a podcast with Milwaukee Brewers star Christian Yelich. Earnhardt Jr. is the son of former racing icon Dale Earnhardt, who is tied with both Richard Petty and Jimmie Johnson for the most NASCAR championships (7) in the sport's history.

item.title: Gill explained that Tellem warned the Nets that Bryant …
item.description: Speaking with Brandon “Scoop B” Robinson on The Pull Up with Scoop B, Gill recalled being in the room with agent Arn Tellem while the Nets debated using the No. 7 pick on Bryant. Tellem also represented Bryant and strongly opposed New Jersey drafting the teenage guard directly out of high school. “I was right there when they were thinking about drafting Kobe,” Gill said. “Arn is on the phone with John Calipari and he’s saying, ‘Look. We’re going to take Kobe at the 7th pick…’” Gill explained tha

item.title: Bulls also want to interview Lamar Skeeter, Ryan Schmidt
item.description: Jake Fischer: Two more candidates that the Bulls plan to seek permission to interview for Chicago’s coaching search, per sources: Charlotte assistant Lamar Skeeter, and Atlanta assistant Ryan Schmidt. More NBA from @TheSteinLine here:

item.title: What about New Orleans for Gobert? Toronto. They were …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "I just I've always come to the conclusion, if Rudy Gobert is playing 30 to 35 minutes a night, you're not winning a championship. I've thought that for years, I continue to think that, but he's an incredible floor raiser. Like if you're Charlotte, he would be great. If you're New Orleans, think about hiring defensive-minded head coach Jamal Mosley. What about New Orleans for Gobert? Toronto. They were good, but Jakob Poeltl, like no. Toronto's had interest in Gobert for a while.

item.title: Cooper Flagg, Dylan Harper, Kon Knueppel headline All-Rookie First Team
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie First Team: Cedric Coward, @memgrizz VJ Edgecombe, @sixers Cooper Flagg, @dallasmavs Dylan Harper, @spurs Kon Knueppel, @hornets

item.title: “The Cavs got my son Lonzo for this exact moment. Yall …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Lavar Ball on the Cavaliers getting swept by the Knicks “The Cavs got my son Lonzo for this exact moment. Yall didn’t get him for the season, you got him for this right here & look how you get blown out… Charlotte go get them boys” (h/t @SnapshotKyle)

item.title: #Hornets pre-draft workout group today includes Nick …
item.description: Rod Boone: #Hornets pre-draft workout group today includes Nick Boyd (Wisconsin), Wyatt Fricks (Marshall), Nate Johnson (Kansas State) and Tre White (Kansas), per league sources.

item.title: Hometown power couple Stephen and Ayesha Curry and …
item.description: Hometown power couple Stephen and Ayesha Curry and Michelin-starred restaurateur Michael Mina are teaming up to bring three new luxury dining options to uptown Charlotte late this year. Multi-phase renovations are under way at The Ritz-Carlton, Charlotte. By winter, the premium hotel will debut three experience-led concepts, according to a news release and interviews given exclusively to CharlotteFive ahead of Thursday’s announcement:

item.title: The collaboration marks a homecoming for the Currys — …
item.description: The collaboration marks a homecoming for the Currys — who met while growing up in Charlotte and visit regularly — and solidifies their ties with the The MINA Group, which operates the original location of The Eighth Rule in San Francisco and pairs with Sweet July at the Regent Santa Monica Beach. “The partnerships that we’re entering into with Michael Mina and Ayesha and Steph Curry are not just exciting for The Ritz-Carlton, Charlotte — I think they are super exciting for Charlotte as a whole,”

item.title: A defendant in the federal NBA gambling case told a …
item.description: A defendant in the federal NBA gambling case told a judge today that he paid an NBA player to change his performance so he could bet on him. The player, a federal prosecutor later clarified, was former Charlotte Hornets and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier. Through his attorney, Rozier disputed the claim. The defendant, Marves Fairley, made the admission Thursday as he pleaded guilty to seven felony counts in two separate cases brought by the Department of Justice over the last year. Federal prosec

item.title: More on this:
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: More on this: — While DOJ named sportsbooks as victims in original indictment, today they said Rozier and others conspired to defraud NBA and the Hornets “through bribery and kickbacks.” — Charges came hours after another defendant told judge he paid NBA player to limit his play

item.title: Hornets guarantee Moussa Diabate's contract for 2026-27
item.description: Ohm Youngmisuk: The Charlotte Hornets have guaranteed Moussa Diabate’s contract for next season according to league sources. It was non-guaranteed. Diabate won the NBA Hustle Award this season.

item.title: Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts …
item.description: Tobias Bass: Miami’s Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts with the Jazz, Suns, Spurs, Lakers, Warriors, Clippers, Knicks He will also workout with the Hornets, Bucks, Heat, Kings, Raptors, Celtics, Pistons, Magic, Nuggets and Trail Blazers

item.title: Noa Essengue: "Oh yes, and I would say first of all …
item.description: Joakim Noah: "Did you learn things, are there teammates who taught you things, who helped you for this year?" Noa Essengue: "Oh yes, and I would say first of all the two who helped me a lot at the time of my injury were Coby White and Ayo Dosunmu. Because it is two guys who went through this operation too. So talking with them, talking about how rehabilitation was, how long it takes, if it was hard to come back afterwards, for this injury, they helped me a lot."

item.title: Terry Rozier will forfeit most of his 2025-26 $26.6 million salary due to alleged role in sports gambling
item.description: Terry Rozier was in violation of his NBA contract and will forfeit most of his $26.6 million salary for the 2025-26 season due to his alleged role in a sports gambling scheme while he was with the Charlotte Hornets, an arbitrator ruled last month. The ruling was included in a court filing released Wednesday.

item.title: The same arbitrator initially said the NBA could not …
item.description: The same arbitrator initially said the NBA could not withhold Rozier’s salary this season as he faces federal charges from the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District of New York. The NBA put him on unpaid administrative leave in October, days after Rozier, then with the Miami Heat, was arrested. He and the National Basketball Players Association filed a grievance, and an arbitrator ruled at the time that Rozier should be paid despite being on leave.

item.title: Rozier and the NBA returned to the arbitrator this …
item.description: Rozier and the NBA returned to the arbitrator this spring after the league said he should not be paid, as the conditions of his bond — which don’t allow him to travel across the country or be in contact with the Heat and Hornets — prevented him from fulfilling his contract. The union and Rozier again appealed, but this time the arbitrator ruled in the league’s favor. Rozier and the NBA returned to the arbitrator this spring after the league said he should not be paid, as the conditions of his bo

item.title: On Wednesday, a lawyer for Rozier asked the judge …
item.description: On Wednesday, a lawyer for Rozier asked the judge overseeing his case to change the conditions of his release because Rozier still hopes to play in the NBA and believes those terms are impacting his ability to do so. The Eastern District attorney’s office removed the Heat from his no-contact list late last month, the filing released Wednesday said, after Miami released him in April, but the Hornets remain. The NBA had asked federal prosecutors to prevent Rozier from communicating with anyone in

item.title: A lawsuit seeking over $3 million from Charlotte …
item.description: A lawsuit seeking over $3 million from Charlotte Hornets star LaMelo Ball is moving closer to trial, court records show. The suit is tied to allegations that Ball injured a child's foot while leaving Spectrum Center following a scrimmage in 2023. The alleged incident happened following the "Purple and Teal Day at the Hive," a free activity event hosted by the team on Oct. 7, 2023. According to the suit, Ball drove off in a "grossly negligent and reckless manner" after fans swarmed his car at a t

item.title: Kings didn't discuss Domantas Sabonis trade with Hornets at trade deadline
item.description: James Ham: I checked in with a very high-ranking source, league source, someone that is not on the peripheral, someone who is in the know and who has a very good grasp on what's happening in Sacramento. And the answer came pretty quickly. According to my source, there was no conversation between the Kings and the Charlotte Hornets regarding Domantas Sabonis at the trade deadline. That is, according to my source, not accurate. In addition, the idea that the Kings would trade Sabonis in a straight

item.title: The Pelicans have named David Cosgrave as Vice …
item.description: New Orleans Pelicans: The Pelicans have named David Cosgrave as Vice President of Health & Performance and Joe Sharpe as Head Athletic Trainer Cosgrave spent over 12 years in the Premier League, contributing across performance environments at Tottenham Hotspur, Liverpool FC, Stoke City, and Fulham FC. He later played a key role at FC Copenhagen during a highly successful period, contributing to six Danish titles and multiple deep UEFA competition runs between 2014 and 2021. Sharpe brings over

item.title: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of …
item.description: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of the headlines, as the projected No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft, Keba Keita has consistently impressed NBA teams throughout the predraft process. “Keba is on the rise,” his agent, Keith Kreiter, said. “He’s been outplaying bigs who are projected to go in the first round. His physicality and ability to guard one through five makes him a no-brainer for the mid-to-late second round.” He’s worked out with the Utah Jazz, Washington Wizards, Charlo

item.title: Hornets likes to shop Miles Bridges this offseason
item.description: Charlotte is expected to make Miles Bridges available in trade talks in conjunction with the upcoming draft, league sources say, after essentially doing so at the February trade deadline.

item.title: Hornets have 'some interest' in Domantas Sabonis
item.description: Per league sources, Sacramento and Charlotte have had recent talks about Kings big man Domantas Sabonis. While a deal is not imminent and appears unlikely before the June 23 draft, sources say the Hornets have some interest in the 30-year-old, three-time All-Star. The problem for now, it seems, is that the Kings have been hoping to land one of Charlotte’s two first-round picks (they have Nos. 14 and 18) in the deal, and the Hornets have signaled a desire to retain their picks and discuss Sabonis

item.title: Hornets, Kings discussed Domantas Sabonis trade that would have sent Miles Bridges, Josh Green, draft capital to Sacramento
item.description: The Sacramento Kings and Charlotte Hornets had exploratory trade talks surrounding Domantas Sabonis, league sources told HoopsHype. One framework loosely kicked around included Charlotte’s Miles Bridges, Josh Green, and a potential first-round draft pick going to Sacramento in exchange for Sabonis, sources said. Charlotte has two first-round selections in the upcoming draft, including the 14th and 18th picks. However, Charlotte has been resistant to including one of those first-round picks thus

item.title: The federal prosecutor, Kaitlin T. Farrell, said she …
item.description: The judge, LaShann DeArcy Hall, said she wants to hear from the NBA before she decides what to do. The Hornets have taken no position on the request, a lawyer for the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District said. The federal prosecutor, Kaitlin T. Farrell, said she believes the league prefers that the no-contact requirement not be lifted and that he not be allowed to play. Hall gave the league until Monday to file a letter to the Eastern District with its preference.

item.title: Sources say Charlotte, though, is not currently …
item.description: There have been multiple reports this week about Sacramento and Charlotte discussing Domantas Sabonis trade frameworks, but sources say those conversations were preliminary in nature and reflective of the Kings' desire to acquire the Hornets' No. 14 or No. 18 pick. Sources say Charlotte, though, is not currently willing to surrender either pick. I'm told that the Kings also have interest in Toronto's No. 19 selection, but it is unclear whether the Raptors, who showed trade interest in Sabonis b

item.title: Terry Rozier's trial date set for February 2027
item.description: Terry Rozier has pleaded not guilty to all four charges. The judge set a Feb. 8, 2027, date for trial. The NBA, however, put Rozier on leave soon after he was arrested last October. It tried to withhold his $26.6 million salary as well, but Rozier and the NBPA appealed the decision to an arbitrator, who ruled in his favor. But the league ultimately prevailed this spring when it again decreased Rozier’s salary, saying the conditions of his bond set in mid-December prevented him from fulfilling hi

item.title: A federal judge has asked the NBA to weigh in on …
item.description: A federal judge has asked the NBA to weigh in on whether Terry Rozier should have a chance to play in the league next season. Rozier is not allowed to be in contact with the Charlotte Hornets while he awaits a trial for charges that he took a bribe to manipulate his performance during a 2023 game while he was with the team — one of four federal counts stemming from an investigation into alleged illegal sports gambling. A lawyer for Rozier has asked the Department of Justice and the judge to lift

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg so far has worked out for the …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: Yaxel Lendeborg so far has worked out for the Warriors, Thunder, Heat, Bucks, Hornets and Mavs. He has workouts with the Clippers and Hawks left

item.title: Rival teams say the Hornets are looking for frontcourt …
item.description: Rival teams say the Hornets are looking for frontcourt upgrades via both the draft and trade market, and could potentially move one of their two firsts (14 and 18) for the right player. Charlotte has several players on movable expiring contracts, including Miles Bridges, giving them flexibility to make deals.

item.title: The NBA and Charlotte Hornets told federal prosecutors …
item.description: The NBA and Charlotte Hornets told federal prosecutors they prefer veteran guard Terry Rozier continue to be prohibited from contacting current and former members of the Hornets while he remains out on bond and awaiting trial on four charges stemming from a federal investigation into alleged NBA gambling and insider information trading. Last week, a federal judge asked the league to weigh in as she contemplated whether to remove those bond restrictions at the request of Rozier and his lawyer, wh

item.title: Different possibilities are being discussed, including …
item.description: Among the most interesting outcomes that will affect the rest of the NBA will be how an expansion draft is structured -- the last time was in 2004, and it was for just one team, the Charlotte Bobcats. At that time, teams were able to protect eight players and lose only one. There is also the question of how the league will realign the conferences. Different possibilities are being discussed, including moving one team from the Western Conference to the East -- with Memphis, Minnesota and New Orle

item.title: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA …
item.description: Elliot Clough: NEW: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets, Chicago Bulls, Dallas Mavericks, Memphis Grizzlies, Oklahoma City Thunder, Toronto Raptors. He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: Stirtz has worked out with the following teams:
item.description: Elliot Clough: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets Chicago Bulls Dallas Mavericks Memphis Grizzlies Oklahoma City Thunder Toronto Raptors He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: Hornets have Nic Claxton on their radar
item.description: With Charlotte considering a starting-caliber center upgrade, other centers besides Sabonis are on their radar, including Brooklyn Nets center Nic Claxton, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, it’s worth noting that the Nets have expressed interest in Hornets free agent guard Coby White in the past when he was on the Chicago Bulls, sources said. In other Hornets news, assistant coach Zach Peterson is a candidate to become the franchise’s next head coach of their G League affiliate, the Gr

item.title: Orlando Magic will add DJ Bakker as an assistant …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Orlando Magic will add DJ Bakker as an assistant coach. He was a Hornets assistant coach and head coach of the Greensboro Swarm in the G League. He’s been an assistant for the Bucks and Pistons. Bakker and Orlando Magic coach Sean Sweeney worked together in Detroit.

item.title: Other players regarded as potentially available and …
item.description: Other players regarded as potentially available and said to interest Detroit include Charlotte's Coby White, Oklahoma City's Isaiah Joe and possibly even Sacramento's Zach LaVine now that LaVine is heading into the final year of a massive contract with a $49 million player option that carries a June 29 deadline to be activated.

item.title: Charlotte, armed with picks Nos. 14 and 18, is …
item.description: Charlotte, armed with picks Nos. 14 and 18, is believed to be exploring avenues to move up, sources said, along with Chicago at No. 15 (which also owns the No. 4 pick) and Memphis at No. 16 (which also owns the 3rd overall selection).

item.title: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they could trade. They could trade their 2029 first-round pick on the condition that their 2027 first-round pick (top-14 protected) conveys to Charlotte. They could try to regain control of it in a trade with the Hornets.

item.title: “He’s not afraid to be like, ‘I’m gonna go make a …
item.description: As Kon Knueppel listened to Jalen Brunson’s response, he thought about the times he’s watched him play with that exact demeanor in bigger moments, unafraid of the potential for a mistake. “He’s not afraid to be like, ‘I’m gonna go make a play. I’m gonna make an aggressive play. And if I make a mistake, it’s gonna be an aggressive mistake,’” Knueppel said. “He’s not afraid to lose,” Knueppel added. “And to win you can’t be afraid to lose.”

item.title: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. …
item.description: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. That’s 150 first-round picks. First question: did they make an impact for more than a month or two for the team that drafted them? The obvious choices are Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball (2020); Cade Cunningham (2021) and Stephon Castle (2024). Throw in guys like Onyeka Okongwu (2020) and Anthony Black (2023) who clearly are difference makers for the teams that drafted them or traded for them on draft night. How many of that 150 total made t

item.title: Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma trade candidates for Bucks?
item.description: With Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis gone, all eyes turn to holdovers Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma as trade candidates for the Bucks. Turner has always known that if Antetokounmpo was ever traded, he’d be a strong trade candidate, HoopsHype has learned. During his Pacers tenure, Turner was linked to the Hornets, Pelicans, and Lakers in trade rumors at various points. Those teams could all make sense now, and possibly the Celtics. Turner, 30, is still owed $83.55 million, including a player

item.title: Warriors, Hornets discussed picks swap
item.description: The Warriors have talked with the Charlotte Hornets about the possibility of swapping draft picks, with Golden State’s No. 11 pick going to Charlotte for Nos. 14 and 18, contingent on certain players being on the board, league sources told HoopsHype. Charlotte has signaled a willingness to move up in the draft, seemingly to leapfrog Oklahoma City, and executives across the league are wondering which player the Hornets could be targeting.

item.title: Charlotte takes Hannes Steinbach at No. 14
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Charlotte Hornets will select Hannes Steinbach with the No. 14 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Hornets taking Christian Anderson at No. 18
item.description: Dan Woike: Hearing Christian Anderson could be the choice here at 18

item.title: Hannes Steinbach and Christian Anderson are meeting …
item.description: Rod Boone: Newest members of the #Hornets have arrived in town. Hannes Steinbach and Christian Anderson are meeting with some Charlotte youth at a local church.

item.title: Xavier Tillman signs with Turkish team
item.description: Xavier Tillman is continuing his basketball career overseas. The former Boston Celtics, Memphis Grizzlies, and Charlotte Hornets center has signed with Turkish club Trabzonspor, the organization announced on Wednesday.

item.title: Multiple teams strongly pursuing LaMelo Ball
item.description: Shams Charania: Reporting live for the NBA Draft Show -- the Charlotte Hornets are engaged in LaMelo Ball trade talks as multiple teams strongly pursue the star guard: Multiple teams are strongly pursuing LaMelo Ball right now. The Hornets are engaged, I'm told, and Ball had a strong finish to the season. It mirrored the Hornets rise over the course of the last calendar year. Both sides have been in a good place. Ball does want to be in Charlotte, I'm told, but this is an organization fielding s

item.title: Wolves, Raptors emerge as teams interested in LaMelo Ball
item.description: Marc Stein: The Timberwolves and Raptors have emerged as two of the teams with trade interest in Charlotte’s LaMelo Ball, league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me. More to come …

item.title: Lamelo Ball, sources say, has been looking forward to …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Lamelo Ball, sources say, has been looking forward to this upcoming Hornets season to build off of Charlotte’s momentum from 2025-26, but the Hornets are indeed engaging the trade market for their franchise guard.

item.title: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several …
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Toronto Raptors and Minnesota Timberwolves, sources confirm. Toronto originally expressed trade interest in Ball during the summer of 2025 and renewed talks with the Charlotte Hornets over the past few days.

item.title: Minnesota Timberwolves have expressed a strong desire …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Minnesota Timberwolves have expressed a strong desire to acquire Charlotte Hornets star LaMelo Ball, league sources tell me. Hornets only listening to offers at the moment.

item.title: The Wolves can expand the Julius Randle trade to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Wolves can expand the Julius Randle trade to acquire LaMelo Ball. They'd want to do a 100 percent salary match so they can exceed the first apron, which can be done by including Donte DiVincenzo. Then it's about adding draft picks & young players to incentivize Charlotte.

item.title: If the Minnesota Timberwolves trade for LaMelo Ball, …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: If the Minnesota Timberwolves trade for LaMelo Ball, keep an eye on the three-team trade that the Wolves agreed to with the Brooklyn Nets and Chicago Bulls becoming an expanded trade to include the Charlotte Hornets. That would allow Minnesota to use Julius Randle and the outgoing salary-match (he'd still end up Brooklyn). Would need other pieces involved too but could work for the teams involved.

item.title: Bucks also expressed interest in LaMelo Ball
item.description: Jake Fischer: Sources say the Milwaukee Bucks have also registered trade interest recently in LaMelo Ball, but that has dated back to when the Bucks looked at him as a potential addition to Giannis Antetokounmpo. Story here: tinyurl.com/4hxz8593

item.title: Hornets listening to offers for LaMelo Ball despite his interest in staying
item.description: Sources say LaMelo Ball has been looking forward to this upcoming season with the team that drafted him No. 3 overall in 2020 to try to build off of Charlotte's momentum from 2025-26. The Hornets went a surprising 44-38 this season, only to fall at the final Play-In Tournament hurdle in Orlando and miss the playoffs for a league-high 10th consecutive season. We're told that the Hornets are nonetheless listening to the offers they're getting despite their recent success under second-year coach Ch

item.title: Max Mackinnon has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with …
item.description: Olgun Uluc: Max Mackinnon has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with the Charlotte Hornets, sources told ESPN. The 6’6 Australian guard averaged 15.7 ppg, 2.6 rpg, and 2.5 apg as a senior at LSU.

item.title: "Well, the team that you're hearing about the most …
item.description: The Minnesota Timberwolves have reportedly emerged as the strongest candidate to pursue the Charlotte Hornets star, with league sources continuing to connect Ball to a potential partnership with Anthony Edwards. Speaking about the situation, MacMahon explained that Minnesota is the team being discussed most frequently among league executives and insiders. "Well, the team that you're hearing about the most from people around the league is the Minnesota Timberwolves. And it's no secret," he said p

item.title: "If the cost is Gobert, I can't see how they go …
item.description: Brian Windhorst on the Timberwolves potentially trading Rudy Gobert for LaMelo Ball: "If the cost is Gobert, I can't see how they go forward. They won the series against the Nuggets because of Gobert. There is a height and size race in the Western Conference right now. So I'm not sure what the pathway is. I guess it's possible that they could expand the Julius Randle trade to a multiple team trade to open this door, but I do think Minnesota feels like they have to look at this, and I think Charl

item.title: Hornets waiving Tosan Evbuomwan
item.description: Marc Stein: The Hornets are waiving forward Tosan Evbuomwan, league sources say. Evbuomwan has averaged 7.4 points, 3.6 rebounds, and 1.4 assists in 50 career NBA games and is coming off a G League Finals MVP performance this past season.

item.title: The Timberwolves were one of several teams to express …
item.description: After ESPN reported Wednesday night that the Charlotte Hornets were fielding offers for Ball, the Timberwolves were one of several teams to express interest in trading for the star point guard, team and league sources told The Athletic. The people spoke on the condition of anonymity because discussions were still in the very early stages.

item.title: The Charlotte Hornets are signing Michael Ajayi to a …
item.description: Tony Jones: The Charlotte Hornets are signing Michael Ajayi to a two-year two-way contract, league sources tell The Athletic. A forward out of Butler, Ajayi was one of the best players in the Big East Conference

item.title: Wyatt Fricks and Alabama guard Latrell Wrightsell Jr. …
item.description: ‪Rod Boone‬: Marshall forward Wyatt Fricks and Alabama guard Latrell Wrightsell Jr. have agreed to Exhibit 10 contracts with the #Hornets, according their agency Edge Sports International.

item.title: Timberwolves trade big haul, including Naz Reid, to Charlotte for star point guard LaMelo Ball
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Charlotte Hornets are trading star guard LaMelo Ball and Josh Green to the Minnesota Timberwolves for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected first-round pick, three first-round pick swaps (2028, 2029, 2030) and three second-round picks (2029, 2032, 2033), sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Timberwolves send a haul to the Hornets for a …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Timberwolves send a haul to the Hornets for a star-studded new backcourt in Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball -- the Nos. 1 and 3 picks in 2020. Charlotte didn't shop Ball in the market, but listened and received multiple rich offers before filling a significant need in the frontcourt with Reid. Timberwolves co-owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez enter the luxury tax for the third straight year -- and the franchise makes a big move to fill the point guard spot.

item.title: The Minnesota Timberwolves are acquiring Charlotte …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: The Minnesota Timberwolves are acquiring Charlotte Hornets star LaMelo Ball in exchange for for Naz Reid, a first round pick, three first round pick swaps and three second round picks.

item.title: Charlotte is indeed now trading LaMelo Ball to …
item.description: Marc Stein: Charlotte is indeed now trading LaMelo Ball to Minnesota, league sources say, after @TheSteinLine revealed Minnesota’s interest last night. The Hornets have successfully pried Naz Reid away from the Wolves and will now try to retain Pistons target Coby White in free agency.

item.title: The easiest path could be to fold this deal into the …
item.description: Keith Smith: Multiple ways the LaMelo Ball trade could be completed, but unless other parts of this emerge later, the easiest path could be to fold this deal into the previously agreed to trade with the Nets, Bulls and Timberwolves. That would solve salary-matching issues for the Wolves.

item.title: Timebrwolves will need to fold the LaMelo Ball trade …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Timebrwolves will need to fold the LaMelo Ball trade with Nets/Bulls trade since their post-trade payroll is above the first apron. Julius Randle & Naz Reid for LaMelo Ball & Josh Green is a 100 percent salary match. They're now hard capped to second apron for aggregation.

item.title: Hornets have extracted an undeniable haul from …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Hornets have extracted an undeniable haul from Minnesota to land Naz Reid plus an unprotected first-round pick in 2033 as well as three first-round pick swaps and three second-round picks. It is expected this trade will be folded into the three-team Julius Randle deal.

item.title: Hornets reduce their payroll by $21 million by trading …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Hornets reduce their payroll by $21 million by trading LaMelo Ball and Josh Green for Naz Reid. Josh Green for Naz Reid is a clean salary match, allowing them to create a $40.8 million trade exception for Ball. They also have 12 future first-round picks through 2033.

item.title: Ball was somebody the Wolves long thought could be a …
item.description: ‪‪Chris Hine‬: LaMelo Ball was somebody the Wolves long thought could be a great backcourt mate with Anthony Edwards and think he can be a winning player, despite public perception. The question was if he’d ever become available. He did, and here we are.

item.title: LaMelo Ball and Josh Green are going to the Wolves for …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov‬: LaMelo Ball and Josh Green are going to the Wolves for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected 1st round pick, swaps in 2028, 2029, and 2030, and 3 second round picks, a league source confirmed. Shams was first on it. A huge swing for both teams.

item.title: “He’s (Ant) is tired of being double teamed. They get …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Brian Windhorst on LaMelo the Minnesota: “He’s (Ant) is tired of being double teamed. They get him a dynamic scorer in the backcourt. You throw a double at Ant Edwards with Lamelo Ball on the court, you are playing with fire. Lamelo will drop 10 3s on you in a blink of an eye”

item.title: Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez enter the luxury tax for …
item.description: ‪Shams Charania Tweets‬: Timberwolves co-owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez enter the luxury tax for the third straight year -- and the franchise makes a big move to fill the point guard spot.

item.title: $75 million in room below the luxury tax line. Enough …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Charlotte Hornets salary cap situation after trading LaMelo Ball and Josh Green for Naz Reid and picks. $75 million in room below the luxury tax line. Enough room to re-sign Coby White, use the entirety of their $40.8 million trade exception, and $15 million mid-level.

item.title: Timberwolves cap situation after trading for LaMelo …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Minnesota Timberwolves cap situation after trading for LaMelo Ball. He has a small trade bonus that increases his salary to the $41.25 million max. They have $11 million under second apron hard cap to fill 4 roster spots. They will no longer have a $33 million trade exception.

item.title: Timberwolves project to be $4.7M over the luxury tax …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Minnesota Timberwolves project to be $4.7M over the luxury tax after adding LaMelo Ball and Josh Green. Minnesota will be $3.3M under the first apron and could trigger a first-apron hard cap (taking back more salary than they send out or using the NTMLE as a TPE). 1/2

item.title: @boogiecousins says trading for LaMelo Ball is a great …
item.description: Run It Back: . @boogiecousins says trading for LaMelo Ball is a great move for the Minnesota Timberwolves 🔥 "They've been searching for a point guard to play with Anthony Edwards... could be really special, something the league hasn't seen." @MichelleDBeadle | @ChandlerParsons | @TeamLou23

item.title: Wolves have been trying to get LaMelo Ball for two …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves have been trying to get LaMelo Ball for two years. They paid a price with Naz, but there is elation throughout the building about pairing Melo and Anthony Edwards.

item.title: “He’s going to make everybody better. That was the …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: “He’s going to make everybody better. That was the thing that was missing." -- Wolves source on the addition of LaMelo Ball.

item.title: "This is a plus for LaMelo and the Minnesota …
item.description: Run It Back: Boogie Cousins thinks LaMelo Ball's immaturity issues will go away playing with Anthony Edwards. "This is a plus for LaMelo and the Minnesota Timberwolves." @MichelleDBeadle | @boogiecousins | @ChandlerParsons | @TeamLou23

item.title: Without draft picks, they might have to include …
item.description: Sam Quinn: If Minnesota is keeping Donte DiVincenzo, they somehow have to turn Josh Green's $14.7 million salary into a playable power forward. Without draft picks, they might have to include Terrence Shannon to do so. But that's the next order of business here.

item.title: Coby White intends to sign three-year deal to return to Hornets
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Coby White intends to sign a three-year, $74 million deal to return to the Charlotte Hornets, sources tell ESPN. White was a major priority for the Hornets and now steps into a new starting role in the backcourt.

item.title: Timberwolves had discussions with Celtics on potential Jaylen Brown trade before LaMelo Ball deal
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves did have discussions with the Celtics on Jaylen Brown as well, sources told The Athletic. But they saw LaMelo Ball as fitting more of what they needed, for likely less compensation going out.

item.title: Hornets officials worked through White's free agency …
item.description: Shams Charania: Hornets officials worked through White's free agency with CAA Basketball agents Steven Heumann and Ty Sullivan -- putting the final touches on a new contract hours after the departure of LaMelo Ball.

item.title: Kylan Boswell agrees to a two-way contract with Charlotte
item.description: Illinois Men’s Basketball: From Champaign to Charlotte. Kylan Boswell signs a two-way contract with the Hornets.

item.title: Cameron Matthews will play for the Charlotte Hornets …
item.description: Owen O’Connor: Source: Cameron Matthews will play for the Charlotte Hornets at NBA Summer League in Las Vegas. Matthews is also strongly in the conversation for one of Charlotte’s two-way contracts as an elite versatile defender, according to a league source.

item.title: Hornets plan to keep Naz Reid
item.description: According to ESPN's Shams Charania, the Hornets plan to keep Reid on their roster despite already receiving trade inquiries for the versatile big man. "They already got some trade calls in on [Naz Reid], but the plan is right now to keep him. This is a team that could still shift the roster around a little bit if they want," Charania reported on ESPN's SportsCenter.

item.title: White, who was critical in Charlotte's overtime win …
item.description: White, who was critical in Charlotte's overtime win against Miami and played just 17 minutes in a 121-90 blowout elimination loss to Orlando, had also communicated a strong desire ahead of free agency to be a starter in the league whether in Charlotte or elsewhere, sources say.

item.title: Hornets in the mix for Jaylen Brown?
item.description: Mike Lacett: I’m hearing the @hornets are one of these teams

item.title: I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "They were in on Jaylen Brown as well. It was made clear by the Celtics that he was going to be available and the Wolves were trying to have conversations about what it would take to get Jaylen Brown. I think that they looked at those two, Jaylen Brown and LaMelo Ball, and Brown, just because he's won a championship, he's a little older, he was top-six MVP this year, I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown than what the Hornets wanted for Ball."

item.title: The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point …
item.description: Ball, 24, is under contract for the next three seasons and is set to make $40.8 million next year as a running mate for Anthony Edwards in Minnesota. The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point guard and league sources confirmed to MassLive that the Celtics did not pursue a deal for the former Hornets guard.

item.title: Tim MacMahon: "I texted several general managers a …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "I texted several general managers a simple question, what do you think of the LaMelo deal? And one replied one sentence, Tim is a riverboat gambler. And this is big-time, high-stakes gambling that he's doing right here. And you understand the swing that he's taking, you understand the hole in the roster it's creating. Chris Finch is also one of the most creative coaches in the league and he's going to earn his money figuring out how to make this work."

item.title: Jon Krawczynski: "I think Tim Connelly and Chris Finch …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "I think Tim Connelly and Chris Finch have long believed that LaMelo Ball is a perfect fit for Anthony Edwards in that backcourt. And I know that over the last several years, they have checked in on LaMelo and Charlotte a couple of times. Each time they were turned away, and so something has happened in the last couple of days or weeks to get the Charlotte Hornets to a point where they were comfortable parting with LaMelo Ball."

item.title: Charlotte also quickly resumed negotiations with …
item.description: Charlotte also quickly resumed negotiations with White, who sources say initially turned down an offer following the Finals in search of a starting role, now with definite clarity on his potential future under Lee. (White's three-year, $74 million deal, as Yahoo reported earlier, is fully guaranteed with no player or team options.)

item.title: The once supersized Wolves are now looking very thin …
item.description: The once supersized Wolves are now looking very thin in the frontline. They will have a difficult time finding an upgrade with no draft picks left to trade. They might need to part with one of Terrence Shannon Jr. or Joan Beringer to get a team interested. Otherwise, they’re looking at starting a lineup featuring LaMelo Ball, Ayo Dosunmu, Anthony Edwards, Jaden McDaniels, and Rudy Gobert. The Wolves would have limited options for upgrading the frontcourt. They will no longer create a $33 million

item.title: Celtics wanted Rudy Gobert, Naz Reid, first-round pick and pick swaps from Timberwolves for Jaylen Brown
item.description: The Timberwolves explored multiple avenues to add another star next to Edwards in the days leading up to landing Ball. There were discussions with the Chicago Bulls about Josh Giddey. They had talks with the Boston Celtics about Jaylen Brown, a team source said, but the Celtics were looking for a package including Gobert, Reid and similar pick compensation that went to Charlotte for Ball, team sources said. That was just too much for the Wolves to pay for a soon-to-be 30-year-old forward who, wh

item.title: Word had started to circulate on Wednesday night that …
item.description: Word had started to circulate on Wednesday night that the Hornets were seriously entertaining offers on Ball, and the Timberwolves wasted no time. The final offer for Ball was prepared and sent to owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez for review and approval early Thursday morning, according to multiple team sources that spoke on the condition of anonymity because the trade has not been officially announced.

item.title: And immediately gave the green light, team sources …
item.description: Lore and Rodriguez looked at the offer — former Sixth Man of the Year and Timberwolves cult hero Naz Reid, an unprotected first-round pick in 2033 and three first-round pick swaps (2028, 2029, 2030) and three second-round picks (2029, 2032, 2033) to Charlotte for Ball and swingman Josh Green — and immediately gave the green light, team sources said.

item.title: “We needed a guy like LaMelo to make everybody …
item.description: Ball averaged 20.1 points, 7.1 assists and 4.8 rebounds last season. There are questions about his decision-making and the seriousness with which he takes the game, but the Wolves love his size, shot creation and playmaking. Maybe even more importantly, the Hornets were 11.6 points per 100 possessions better on offense with Ball on the floor last season, per Cleaning The Glass. Kon Knueppel, Charlotte’s terrific rookie, made 46 percent of his 3-pointers when Ball was on the court. That number di

item.title: Reid was preparing to become the full-time starter at …
item.description: Just a few days earlier, Reid was preparing to become the full-time starter at power forward for the first time in his career when the Timberwolves traded Randle to Brooklyn. But the Hornets insisted on Reid being in the trade, sources said.

item.title: "It was almost unanimously that the Charlotte Hornets …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "It was almost unanimously that the Charlotte Hornets were the biggest winner. There is a lot of respect for Tim Connelly. People call him a riverboat gambler. I had some people say I didn't like the Rudy Gobert deal, and that worked out great for the Timberwolves. So, with those caveats, the almost consensus opinion of the people I talked to around the league was LaMelo Ball had next to no value a year ago. Jeff Peterson, the Charlotte Hornets general manager, cleaned him up and a

item.title: A couple league sources confirmed that the Raptors …
item.description: That doesn’t mean that they weren’t or won’t be in the mix for other players. A couple league sources confirmed that the Raptors were interested in getting involved in talks with the Charlotte Hornets for mercurial point guard LaMelo Ball, a player Webster has always liked, which is one of the reasons he was intrigued by the Hornets' top job before current Charlotte GM Jeff Peterson was hired in 2024.

item.title: Gueye is set to be traded to the Minnesota …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Chicago Bulls have picked up their $2.4M team option for Mouhamadou Gueye, a league source told @spotrac. Gueye's contract is now fully non-guaranteed until January 7. Gueye is set to be traded to the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of the Bulls-Wolves-Nets-Hornets trade.

item.title: LaMelo Ball ranked second in the NBA (behind Jokic) in …
item.description: Jeremias Engelmann: Interesting wrinkle of the LaMelo trade: According to an analysis I'm running for a future article, LaMelo Ball ranked second in the NBA (behind Jokic) in raising his teammates TS% Expect everyone on the Hornets to shoot a little worse next season, and the Wolves to shoot better

item.title: Jaylen Brown trade suitors: Blazers, Raptors, Nuggets, Nets, Hornets, Hawks, Clippers
item.description: Brown has drawn trade interest from various teams across the league, including the Portland Trail Blazers, Toronto Raptors, Denver Nuggets, Brooklyn Nets, Charlotte Hornets, Atlanta Hawks, and Los Angeles Clippers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Celtics still interested in Naz Reid after trade to Hornets
item.description: Reid once again was brought up in talks for Brown after joining the Hornets, HoopsHype has learned. Charlotte has also gauged the trade market value of forward Miles Bridges, who’s on an expiring $22.83 million deal. His salary would be needed to make any Brown trade work with Charlotte. With that said, exploratory talks with the Hornets have cooled.

item.title: Vladimir Radmanovic after forcing Lakers trade to Bobcats: 'Be careful what you wish for'
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic on being traded to Hornets: ‘After being kept on the bench for nearly two months with only 'teaspoon' minutes, I couldn't establish any continuity as a player. It was one of the most frustrating periods of my career. I eventually went to Mitch Kupchak and told him, 'If this is going to continue, please count me out and look for a trade.' It’s a classic case of 'be careful what you wish for.' I was traded to the Charlotte Bobcats, which was a franchise in total disarray at the

item.title: Miles Bridges to Phoenix
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Charlotte Hornets are trading Miles Bridges, a 2029 first-round pick and a 2027 second-round pick to the Phoenix Suns for Grayson Allen, Royce O'Neale and a 2033 first-round pick, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Suns to extend Miles Bridged contract eventually
item.description: John Gambadoro: This Suns trade for Miles Bridges came about late last night. The plan is that Bridges will not just be here for one year - at some point not sure when they would like to extend him. To replace Grayson Allen the Suns are looking at 7 to 8 free agent shooting guards. So, another move coming here in free agency soon.

item.title: “I think the Cavs might have been involved a little …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “I think the Cavs might have been involved a little bit, I heard in the Miles Bridges discussions,” - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs. Windy notes that the Cavs could trade Max Strus for an upgrade.

item.title: Hornets exercised 2026-27 team option on Pat Connaughton
item.description: Rod Boone: #Hornets are picking up the $3.8 million team option on Pat Connaughton's contract, league sources told @theobserver. The veteran was a huge voice in the locker room this past season and Charlotte even brought him back after he got caught in a roster crunch at trade deadline.

item.title: #Hornets have been active this past week and are …
item.description: Rod Boone: #Hornets have been active this past week and are continuing to work through their free agency plan and offseason roster construction.

item.title: “Did you know LeBron James loves Charlotte?” McGee …
item.description: ESPN personality Ryan McGee -- who just so happens to have deep Carolina roots -- talked about James’ love for Bojangles during a 2024 appearance on the Pat McAfee Show. “Did you know LeBron James loves Charlotte?” McGee asked McAfee during that appearance. “And the reason he loves Charlotte is because Bojangles -- I’m not making this up -- Bojangles chicken. “When LeBron James was a rookie, the deal was he would leave games at the old Hive ... the takeaway food you get on the plane after a game

item.title: The Wolves do not believe they’re anywhere near the …
item.description: The Wolves do not believe they’re anywhere near the front of the line for James’ services, the team and league sources said, especially considering their lack of spending power. After agreeing to terms with Bones Hyland on a one-year, league minimum deal Tuesday, the Timberwolves have two roster spots to fill and just a hair above the league minimum for one of those spots. They could try to trade Josh Green, who was acquired in the Ball trade and has an expiring $14.7 million contract to try to

item.title: Lonzo Ball defends LaMelo Ball's trade to the …
item.description: Lonzo Ball defends LaMelo Ball's trade to the Timberwolves: "I think this is a great fit for him. People are questioning the fit, but I think this is a great fit for him."

item.title: Tobias Harris had interest from the Pistons, Lakers, Clippers, Hawks and Hornets before joining Spurs
item.description: Before agreeing to a two-year, $31 million deal with the Spurs, Tobias Harris received interest from the Pistons, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks, and Charlotte Hornets, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Timberwolves to add shooting big man?
item.description: Following the blockbuster LaMelo Ball trade, NBA executives around the league believe the Minnesota Timberwolves will now look to add a big man who can stretch the floor, with the working assumption that the organization could look to flip the expiring $14.68 million contract of Josh Green or look to dip into their taxpayer mid-level exception with $6.06 million. Minnesota also remains very bullish on 19-year-old center Joan Beringer, who’s expected to have a bigger role this upcoming season.

item.title: Tobias Harris had interest from the Pistons, Lakers, Clippers, Hawks and Hornets before joining Spurs
item.description: Before agreeing to a two-year, $31 million deal with the Spurs, Tobias Harris received interest from the Pistons, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks, and Charlotte Hornets, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Wolves, Warriors interested in Rui Hachimura?
item.description: As the market has developed, sources say Hachimura's representation has now been in contact with Minnesota and Golden State as well. The Timberwolves have a well-chronicled need for a power forward after committing to trade away Randle and Naz Reid so they can pair LaMelo Ball with Anthony Edwards and Hachimura is said to stand as their current top choice at the position.

item.title: Rockets to trade Dorian Finney-Smith to Hornets
item.description: Shams Charania: The Houston Rockets are trading Dorian Finney-Smith and three second-round picks to the Charlotte Hornets, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Charlotte acquires additional draft capital -- it has …
item.description: Shams Charania: Charlotte acquires additional draft capital -- it has 20 second-rounders over the next 7 years and now the second-most tradeable picks in the NBA. The Rockets open roster flexibility after acquisitions of Marcus Smart and Bogdan Bogdanovic, plus create a $13 million trade exception.

item.title: Asked #Hornets summer league coach Blaine Mueller for …
item.description: Rod Boone: Asked #Hornets summer league coach Blaine Mueller for his first impressions of Hannes Steinbach and Christian Anderson Jr. "Really mature offensive games for both guys. Really polished," Mueller said. "Christian's ability to create for himself and others has been very apparent. "And then Hannes as well. His Just such a great feel in terms of his playmaking and passing out of the post."

item.title: The @hornets have signed forward Michael Ajayi and …
item.description: Charlotte Hornets PR: OFFICIAL: the @hornets have signed forward Michael Ajayi and guard Kylan Boswell to Two-Way contracts. In a corresponding move, the team has waived Two-Way forward Tosan Evbuomwan.

item.title: Dorian Finney-Smith unlikely to play for Hornets
item.description: Rod Boone: Dorian Finney-Smith trade is official. #Hornets sent cash considerations to Houston. Unlikely he'll suit up for the #Hornets, per league sources.

item.title: "I think we've got a really, really great team," he …
item.description: Rod Boone: Asked Coby White about stepping into role as a starter with #Hornets and what he thinks of the roster. "I think we've got a really, really great team," he said.

item.title: ...the goal's never been to get to the playoffs one …
item.description: r/CharlotteHornets: 🎙️ Jeff Peterson: "...the goal's never been to get to the playoffs one time and then be out for 7-8 years, and then try to get back again. It's always been about sustainability, and I think the roster we have will give us the best chance moving forward."

item.title: Charlotte Hornets signed Michael Ajayi and Kylan …
item.description: The Charlotte Hornets signed Michael Ajayi and Kylan Boswell to two-year, two-way contracts.

item.title: The #Hornets surprised Coby White this morning with a …
item.description: Danielle Stein: The #Hornets surprised Coby White this morning with a $20,000 check for the Coby White Family Foundation. Coby started tearing up in sharing what this means to him

item.title: Kai Jones: "I requested (Hornets) to waive me because …
item.description: Kai Jones: "I requested (Hornets) to waive me because they were telling me that they didn't think I was ready to come into training camp and from a mental health perspective. So, there were some things that I was doing, like my sleep pattern, and at this time I was going through a lot. So, my great-grandmother had passed away, and that wasn't the only thing. It was also the way that the things that happened with my family after the fact. I was just in a mental place of, damn, I didn't feel as at

item.title: "Mitch Kupchak, he believed in me from the first day. …
item.description: Kai Jones: "Mitch Kupchak, he believed in me from the first day. He told me that he really thought I was going to be a great player in the NBA. And even when I was waived and a free agent, I called him up and I said, Mitch, thank you for everything you did for me. And he told me, I still think you have a chance to come back into the NBA and be a great player. And I think that I did, showing my preseason with the Clippers and my time with the Clippers and the Mavs. I think that I showed that I ca

item.title: Judge denies Terry Rozier's request to remove former Hornets players from no-contact list
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Spicy day in Brooklyn federal court as Terry Rozier made his latest appearance. A federal judge denied his request to remove certain ex-Hornets players + coaches from no contact list. Rozier's new lawyer has asked judge to move the case to South Florida. Trial date still Feb. 8.

item.title: Rozier continues to seek a job with an NBA team. His …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Rozier continues to seek a job with an NBA team. His current bond terms would prevent that because of how NBA views them, his lawyer said, with ex-teammates now on 4 different teams. He's unable to play against or practice with them, per his bond terms. Judge denied the request.

item.title: Miles Bridges, Luke Kennard deals expected to be completed early next week
item.description: John Gambadoro: The Miles Bridges trade and Luke Kennard signing are expected to be completed early next week. The initial trade with Charlotte, Minnesota and Brooklyn should be done this weekend. Phoenix can’t complete their transaction until that trade is completed. There is no risk of it falling apart, just timing.

item.title: “Obviously, he missed a lot of last year because he …
item.description: Meanwhile, Perry also is expecting Sabonis to play a big role in Sacramento’s rebuild following a disappointing 22-60 record during the 2025-26 season, despite previous reports that the Kings were in trade talks with the Charlotte Hornets involving the three-time NBA All-Star center. “Come in and compete just like everybody else,” Perry told reporters. “Obviously, he’s an excellent player. He’s done it for a while in this league. I’ve been in constant contact with him as well in the offseason. J

item.title: The Bulls have announced that they acquired Nic …
item.description: Brett Siegel: The Bulls have announced that they acquired Nic Claxton from the Nets, which means the four-team deal involving Chicago, Brooklyn, Charlotte, and Minnesota that also involves Julius Randle and LaMelo Ball is now COMPLETE.

item.title: Naz Reid, Mouhamadou Gueye, MIN 2033 1st …
item.description: Brett Siegel: Trade details: Hornets: Naz Reid, Mouhamadou Gueye, MIN 2033 1st (unprotected), MIN 2028, 2029, 2030 1st SWAPS, MIN 2029, 2032, 2033 2nds Nets: Julius Randle, Joshua Jefferson (Pick No. 28) Timberwolves: LaMelo Ball, Josh Green, Isaiah Evans (Pick No. 33) Bulls: Nic Claxton

item.title: “I’ve had conversations with Brandon and his …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Hornets executive Jeff Peterson on Brandon Miller extension talks: “I’ve had conversations with Brandon and his representation. They know that we want Brandon here for a very long time. I won’t get into particulars. Bottom line is we want Brandon Miller here for a very long time”

item.title: Calling it "probably the most difficult" decision he …
item.description: Calling it "probably the most difficult" decision he has had to make, Jeff Peterson said the LaMelo Ball trade was done with the goal of building sustained success for the Charlotte Hornets to contend for a title down the road. The Hornets' president of basketball operations met with the media Saturday to explain the surprising decision to trade one of the franchise's most popular players after the trade became official. The Hornets sent Ball and Josh Green to Minnesota for Naz Reid, a 2033 unpr

item.title: "These decisions are challenging at times, but when …
item.description: "These decisions are challenging at times, but when you look at the totality of the season and everything of where we were, it's important to take an honest look in the mirror of where you guys are as a team," Peterson said at the news conference at the Las Vegas summer league. "And that's what I had to do at the end of the season. And it just felt like the goal is never to compete for a play-in spot. The goal isn't to get to the play-in or even the playoffs for one year. "I've said it plenty of

item.title: "I'll say it every day: LaMelo was tremendous in our …
item.description: "It's my job to not just look at half of last season or two-thirds of last season," Peterson said when asked what he saw in the second-half surge that led him to consider trading Ball. "I've been here for a little bit over two years now. So looking at the totality of the picture and also looking at trends along the league. Just making an objective decision in terms of just the timing and being honest with myself of where we really are as it compares to the rest of the league. "I'll say it every

item.title: "I do empathize with the fans and I understand just …
item.description: Still, Ball was the most popular player on the roster because of his exciting and unpredictable playing style. Peterson said he understands why some fans are unhappy with the decision to trade the 24-year-old former All-Star and Rookie of the Year. "I do empathize with the fans and I understand just where they're coming from," Peterson said when asked for his message to the fanbase. "If I'm honest, I love LaMelo. He's a tremendous human being. Of course, he's an enormous talent. He and I have a

item.title: Charles Lee on the off-season trades "Our front office …
item.description: James Plowright: Charles Lee on the off-season trades "Our front office made some moves, I thought they had the long term vision insight... I know some of the moves seem daunting to them (The fans) but it's going to help us in the long run."

item.title: Charles Lee on if anyone has surprised him "I think …
item.description: James Plowright: Charles Lee on if anyone has surprised him "I think our two two-way guys have done a great job out here, Michael (Ajayi) and Kylan (Boswell)"

item.title: We have acquired Grayson Allen, Royce O’Neale, and a …
item.description: Charlotte Hornets: OFFICIAL: We have acquired Grayson Allen, Royce O’Neale, and a first round pick for Miles Bridges and two picks.

item.title: “Look, when I ultimately made the decision to trade …
item.description: And while Peterson may not have been widely known since being hired in 2024, trading Ball shined the NBA spotlight on the thick-skinned GM. “Fortunately, I don’t get on social media. I don’t really follow it or anything like that,” Peterson, who is 37, told Andscape July 11 from the Thomas & Mack Center during NBA summer league. “I do have people who will send me stuff occasionally, and most of the time it’s as a joke. It’s always funny to me how people who don’t have the full context of a situa

item.title: He’s a guy who has changed his body, has changed his …
item.description: Is the acquisition of Naz Reid an underrated move in this trade? Jeff Peterson: Completely agree. He’s someone I’ve always appreciated. He’s one of my favorite stories in the NBA. Go undrafted. He’s finished top five in [NBA] Sixth Man of the Year running three years, and he won it a couple years ago. He’s been in the Western Conference finals a couple times. He’s a guy who has changed his body, has changed his habits, his approach. So, I like to bet on guys like that. Chip on the shoulder, got
